---
title: "Data"
---

All Wordbank data are hosted on the
[datapages.wordbank](https://redivis.com/datapages/datasets/wordbank) Redivis
dataset, versioned and freely downloadable. Tables can be filtered and
exported directly in the table browsers [below](#browse-the-tables); every
visualization on this site also has a *Download CSV* button for its current
view.

Most datasets are shared under a CC-BY license; some carry CC-BY-NC (see the
`license` column of the `datasets` table).

:::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

## Programmatic access

You can access Wordbank data from R using the
[wordbankr](https://github.com/langcog/wordbankr) package (see the
[data access vignette](http://langcog.github.io/wordbankr/)), which provides
tidy tables of instruments, administrations, items, and child-by-word
responses. From Python or other languages, use the
[Redivis API](https://apidocs.redivis.com/) directly against the dataset.

## Versions

Wordbank data are versioned on Redivis: every release is a citable snapshot,
and older versions remain permanently available from the
[dataset's version history](https://redivis.com/datapages/datasets/wordbank).
If you are running reproducible analyses, record the version you used (in
wordbankr: `options(wordbankr.dataset_version = "v1.3")`).

## Wordbank Book

Many analyses of Wordbank data are described in detail in [*Variability and
Consistency in Early Language Learning: The Wordbank
Project*](https://wordbank-book.stanford.edu), which characterizes
vocabulary development across languages. All of the
[code for those analyses](https://github.com/langcog/wordbank-book) is
available, and is a good starting point for item- and group-level analyses
beyond what the site's interactive tools offer.

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}

## Uni-lemmas

Cross-linguistic "uni-lemma" mappings (conceptual glosses linking items
across languages, used by the [Cross-Linguistic
Trajectories](crossling.qmd) tool and the `uni_lemma` column of the `items`
table) are documented in the
[uni-lemma policy](https://github.com/langcog/update_unilemmas/blob/main/unilemma_policy.md);
the mappings are maintained in the
[update_unilemmas](https://github.com/langcog/update_unilemmas) repository.

## Teaching

Wordbank is used in courses on language development and on data analysis:

- An [in-class exercise](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1VnhKdCBOzgxaaCwm3CsTtF-Cu3gfsl7Kgz1CwQ5c_SE/edit)
  and [assignment](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1UOHuF26t19HLPswsVm46ivA7nS98Z3G9ht9GwCtJYJ0/edit)
  exploring vocabulary development, contributed by
  [Caitlin Fausey](https://sites.google.com/site/caitlinmfausey/)
- [Tutorial materials](https://github.com/mikabr/acq-tools) on accessing
  Wordbank (and CHILDES) programmatically, by
  [Michael Frank](https://web.stanford.edu/~mcfrank/) and
  [Mika Braginsky](https://mikabr.io/)

If you use Wordbank in your teaching, we would love to
[hear about it](mailto:wordbank-contact@stanford.edu).

## Infrastructure

Curious how the site works? See the [FAQ](faq.qmd) and the
[wordbank-datapage](https://github.com/langcog/wordbank-datapage) repository.

:::

::::

## Browse the tables

### Administrations

{{< redivis-table datapages.wordbank.administrations >}}

### Item responses

{{< redivis-table datapages.wordbank.item_responses >}}
